Install and set up @e412/rnui in your project.

## Installation

Install the React components and themes packages:

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

Or with pnpm:

```bash
pnpm add @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

## CSS Setup

Import the theme CSS in your app's global stylesheet:

```css
@import '@e412/rnui-themes';
@import 'tw-animate-css';
```

## Tailwind v4 Integration

The theme CSS includes a `@theme inline` block that automatically registers all design tokens with Tailwind v4. Just importing `@e412/rnui-themes` gives you utilities like `bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, etc.

Add the library to your Tailwind content sources:

```css
@source "../node_modules/@e412/rnui-react/dist";
```

## Dark Mode

Toggle dark mode by adding the `dark` class to your `<html>` element:

```html
<html class="dark">
```

The theme automatically switches all color tokens.

## Quick Example

Once installed, import and use components:

```tsx
import { Button } from '@e412/rnui-react'

export function MyApp() {
  return <Button>Click me</Button>
}
```

That's it — your components are ready to use.
A shared UI component library built on shadcn/ui.
A shared UI component library built on shadcn/ui. Ships React components and prebuilt themes.
Expandable content sections that allow users to show and hide related content. Built on Base UI Accordion primitive.

## Installation

```bash
npx @e412/rnui-cli add accordion
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  Accordion,
  AccordionItem,
  AccordionTrigger,
  AccordionContent,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
<Accordion>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Is it accessible?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      Yes. It adheres to the WAI-ARIA Accordion pattern.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

## Examples

### Multiple Items

```tsx
<Accordion>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>What is this component?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that each reveal a section of content.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Is it styled?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      Yes. It comes with default styles that match the design system.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Is it animated?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      Yes. It uses CSS animations for smooth open and close transitions.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

### Open Multiple

Allow multiple items to be open at the same time by setting `openMultiple`.

```tsx
<Accordion openMultiple>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Section One</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Content for section one.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Section Two</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Content for section two.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

### Default Open

Set the default open item with `defaultValue`.

```tsx
<Accordion defaultValue={[0]}>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Open by default</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      This section is expanded on first render.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Collapsed by default</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      This section starts collapsed.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

### Disabled Item

```tsx
<Accordion>
  <AccordionItem>
    <AccordionTrigger>Enabled item</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>This works normally.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem disabled>
    <AccordionTrigger>Disabled item</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>This cannot be toggled.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

### Command Dialog

Use `CommandDialog` to render the command palette in a centered modal dialog.

```tsx
const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(false)

React.useEffect(() => {
  const down = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
    if (e.key === 'k' && (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) {
      e.preventDefault()
      setOpen((open) => !open)
    }
  }
  document.addEventListener('keydown', down)
  return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', down)
}, [])

<CommandDialog open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Command>
    <CommandInput placeholder="Type a command or search..." />
    <CommandList>
      <CommandEmpty>No results found.</CommandEmpty>
      <CommandGroup heading="Actions">
        <CommandItem>New File</CommandItem>
        <CommandItem>New Folder</CommandItem>
      </CommandGroup>
      <CommandSeparator />
      <CommandGroup heading="Navigation">
        <CommandItem>Home</CommandItem>
        <CommandItem>Settings</CommandItem>
      </CommandGroup>
    </CommandList>
  </Command>
</CommandDialog>
```

Feature-rich data grid built on TanStack Table with sorting, filtering, pagination, column resizing, drag-and-drop columns/rows, and virtual scrolling.

## Installation

```bash
npx @e412/rnui-cli add data-grid
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  DataGrid,
  DataGridContainer,
  useDataGrid,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

Additional sub-components are available for specific features:

```tsx
import { DataGridTable } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridTableVirtual } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridTableDnd } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridTableDndRows } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridPagination } from '@e412/rnui-react'

import { DataGridScrollArea } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import {
  useReactTable,
  getCoreRowModel,
  createColumnHelper,
} from '@tanstack/react-table'

const columnHelper = createColumnHelper<User>()

const columns = [
  columnHelper.accessor('name', { header: 'Name' }),
  columnHelper.accessor('email', { header: 'Email' }),
  columnHelper.accessor('role', { header: 'Role' }),
]

function UsersTable() {
  const table = useReactTable({
    data,
    columns,
    getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(),
  })

  return (
    <DataGrid table={table} recordCount={data.length}>
      <DataGridContainer>
        <DataGridTable />
      </DataGridContainer>
    </DataGrid>
  )
}
```

```tsx
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
  DropdownMenuLabel,
  DropdownMenuGroup,
  DropdownMenuShortcut,
  DropdownMenuCheckboxItem,
  DropdownMenuRadioGroup,
  DropdownMenuRadioItem,
  DropdownMenuSub,
  DropdownMenuSubTrigger,
  DropdownMenuSubContent,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenuTrigger render={<Button variant="outline" />}>
    Open Menu
  </DropdownMenuTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuContent>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Profile</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Settings</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuSeparator />
    <DropdownMenuItem>Log out</DropdownMenuItem>
  </DropdownMenuContent>
</DropdownMenu>
```

A composable filter system with operators, field types, and search support for building data filtering interfaces.

## Installation

```bash
npx @e412/rnui-cli add filters
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  Filters,
  createFilter,
  createFilterGroup,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { Filters, createFilter, type Filter, type FilterFieldConfig } from '@e412/rnui-react'

const fields: FilterFieldConfig[] = [
  {
    key: 'status',
    label: 'Status',
    type: 'select',
    options: [
      { value: 'active', label: 'Active' },
      { value: 'inactive', label: 'Inactive' },
    ],
  },
  {
    key: 'name',
    label: 'Name',
    type: 'text',
  },
]

export function FiltersDemo() {
  const [filters, setFilters] = useState<Filter[]>([])

  return (
    <Filters
      filters={filters}
      fields={fields}
      onChange={setFilters}
    />
  )
}
```

## Examples

### Select Filter

```tsx
const fields: FilterFieldConfig[] = [
  {
    key: 'status',
    label: 'Status',
    type: 'select',
    options: [
      { value: 'active', label: 'Active' },
      { value: 'pending', label: 'Pending' },
      { value: 'closed', label: 'Closed' },
    ],
  },
]

<Filters filters={filters} fields={fields} onChange={setFilters} />
```

### Multi-Select Filter

```tsx
const fields: FilterFieldConfig[] = [
  {
    key: 'tags',
    label: 'Tags',
    type: 'multiselect',
    options: [
      { value: 'bug', label: 'Bug' },
      { value: 'feature', label: 'Feature' },
      { value: 'docs', label: 'Documentation' },
    ],
  },
]

<Filters filters={filters} fields={fields} onChange={setFilters} />
```

### Text Filter

```tsx
const fields: FilterFieldConfig[] = [
  {
    key: 'email',
    label: 'Email',
    type: 'text',
    placeholder: 'Enter email...',
  },
]

<Filters filters={filters} fields={fields} onChange={setFilters} />
```

### Solid Variant

```tsx

  filters={filters}
  fields={fields}
  onChange={setFilters}

/>
```

### Small Size with Full Radius

```tsx

  fields={fields}
  onChange={setFilters}
  size="sm"
  radius="full"
/>
```

### With Keyboard Shortcut

```tsx
<Filters
  filters={filters}
  fields={fields}
  onChange={setFilters}
  enableShortcut
  shortcutKey="f"
/>
```

### Grouped Fields

```tsx
const fields: FilterFieldConfig[] = [
  {
    group: 'User',
    fields: [
      { key: 'name', label: 'Name', type: 'text' },
      { key: 'email', label: 'Email', type: 'text' },
    ],
  },
  {
    group: 'Status',
    fields: [
      { key: 'active', label: 'Active', type: 'select', options: [...] },
    ],
  },
]

<Filters filters={filters} fields={fields} onChange={setFilters} />
```

### Custom Trigger

```tsx
<Filters
  filters={filters}
  fields={fields}
  onChange={setFilters}
  trigger={<Button variant="outline">Custom Filter Button</Button>}
/>
```

## Props

### Filters

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `filters` | `Filter<T>[]` | - | Array of active filters |
| `fields` | `FilterFieldsConfig<T>` | - | Field configuration (flat or grouped) |
| `onChange` | `(filters: Filter<T>[]) => void` | - | Called when filters change |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS class |
| `variant` | `"solid" \| "default"` | `"default"` | Visual style variant |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "default" \| "lg"` | `"default"` | Filter chip size |
| `radius` | `"default" \| "full"` | `"default"` | Border radius style |
| `i18n` | `Partial<FilterI18nConfig>` | English defaults | Internationalization overrides |
| `showSearchInput` | `boolean` | `true` | Show search input in the filter menu |
| `trigger` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Custom trigger element |
| `allowMultiple` | `boolean` | `true` | Allow multiple filters at once |
| `menuPopupClassName` | `string` | - | CSS class for the menu popup |
| `enableShortcut` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable keyboard shortcut to open |
| `shortcutKey` | `string` | `"f"` | Keyboard shortcut key |
| `shortcutLabel` | `string` | `"F"` | Label shown for the shortcut |

### FilterFieldConfig

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `key` | `string` | - | Unique field identifier |
| `label` | `string` | - | Display label |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Icon element |
| `type` | `"select" \| "multiselect" \| "text" \| "custom" \| "separator"` | - | Field type |
| `options` | `FilterOption<T>[]` | - | Options for select/multiselect |
| `operators` | `FilterOperator[]` | - | Custom operators for this field |
| `searchable` | `boolean` | - | Enable search within options |
| `placeholder` | `string` | - | Input placeholder text |
| `customRenderer` | `(props: CustomRendererProps<T>) => ReactNode` | - | Custom value renderer |

A form wrapper built on react-hook-form with accessible labels, descriptions, and validation messages.

## Installation

```bash
npx @e412/rnui-cli add form
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  Form,
  FormField,
  FormItem,
  FormLabel,
  FormControl,
  FormDescription,
  FormMessage,
  useFormField,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { useForm } from 'react-hook-form'
import { Form, FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, FormControl, FormMessage } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { Input } from '@e412/rnui-react'

export function FormDemo() {
  const form = useForm({ defaultValues: { email: '' } })

  return (
    <Form {...form}>
      <form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit((data) => console.log(data))}>
        <FormField
          control={form.control}
          name="email"
          render={({ field }) => (
            <FormItem>
              <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
              <FormControl>
                <Input placeholder="you@example.com" {...field} />
              </FormControl>
              <FormMessage />
            </FormItem>
          )}
        />
      </form>
    </Form>
  )
}
```

## Examples

### With Description

```tsx
<FormField
  control={form.control}
  name="username"
  render={({ field }) => (
    <FormItem>
      <FormLabel>Username</FormLabel>
      <FormControl>
        <Input placeholder="johndoe" {...field} />
      </FormControl>
      <FormDescription>This is your public display name.</FormDescription>
      <FormMessage />
    </FormItem>
  )}
/>
```

### With Validation

```tsx
const form = useForm({
  defaultValues: { email: '' },
  resolver: zodResolver(
    z.object({ email: z.string().email('Invalid email address') })
  ),
})

<FormField
  control={form.control}
  name="email"
  render={({ field }) => (
    <FormItem>
      <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
      <FormControl>
        <Input {...field} />
      </FormControl>
      <FormMessage />
    </FormItem>
  )}
/>
```

### Multiple Fields

```tsx
<Form {...form}>
  <form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit(onSubmit)} className="space-y-4">
    <FormField
      control={form.control}
      name="firstName"
      render={({ field }) => (
        <FormItem>
          <FormLabel>First Name</FormLabel>
          <FormControl>
            <Input {...field} />
          </FormControl>
          <FormMessage />
        </FormItem>
      )}
    />
    <FormField
      control={form.control}
      name="lastName"
      render={({ field }) => (
        <FormItem>
          <FormLabel>Last Name</FormLabel>
          <FormControl>
            <Input {...field} />
          </FormControl>
          <FormMessage />
        </FormItem>
      )}
    />
    <Button type="submit">Submit</Button>
  </form>
</Form>
```

## Props

### Form

The `Form` component is a re-export of `FormProvider` from `react-hook-form`. It accepts all `FormProvider` props.

### FormField

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `control` | `Control` | - | Form control from `useForm` |
| `name` | `string` | - | Field name path |
| `render` | `({ field, fieldState, formState }) => ReactNode` | - | Render function for the field |

### FormItem

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS class |

### FormLabel

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS class |

### FormControl

Wraps the form input using Radix `Slot` to merge accessibility attributes (`id`, `aria-describedby`, `aria-invalid`) onto the child element.

### FormDescription

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS class |

### FormMessage

Renders the field error message automatically. Falls back to `children` when no error is present.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS class |

A one-time password input component with individual character slots and separator support.

## Installation

```bash

```

## Import

```tsx
  InputOTP,
  InputOTPGroup,
  InputOTPSlot,
  InputOTPSeparator,
```

A collapsible JSON tree viewer with line numbers, color-coded indentation, smart value rendering, and expand/collapse controls.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

## Import

```tsx
import { JsonViewer } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
const data = {
  name: "John Doe",
  age: 30,
  active: true,
  address: {
    city: "New York",
    zip: "10001",
  },
}

<JsonViewer data={data} />
```

## Examples

### With Title

```tsx
<JsonViewer data={{ status: "ok", code: 200 }} title="API Response" />
```

### Color-coded Indentation

```tsx
<JsonViewer
  data={{ users: [{ name: "Alice" }, { name: "Bob" }] }}
  showColorIndent
/>
```

### Without Line Numbers

```tsx
<JsonViewer data={{ key: "value" }} showLineNumbers={false} />
```

### Default Expanded to a Depth

```tsx
<JsonViewer
  data={{ a: { b: { c: { d: "deep" } } } }}
  defaultExpanded={2}
/>
```

### Double-click to Collapse

```tsx
<JsonViewer data={{ items: [1, 2, 3] }} collapseOn="doubleClick" />
```

### Custom Truncation

```tsx
<JsonViewer
  data={{ list: Array.from({ length: 50 }, (_, i) => i) }}
  truncation={{ enabled: true, itemsPerArray: 10 }}
/>
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `data` | `Record<string, any>` | -- | **Required.** The JSON data object to display. |
| `title` | `string` | -- | Optional title shown in the viewer header. |
| `showLineNumbers` | `boolean` | `true` | Show line numbers on the left side. |
| `showColorIndent` | `boolean` | `false` | Use color-coded indent guides instead of plain borders. |
| `collapseOn` | `"click" \| "doubleClick"` | `"click"` | Interaction to toggle node expansion. |
| `defaultExpanded` | `boolean \| number` | `false` | Initial expansion state. `true` expands all, a number expands to that depth. |
| `truncation` | `{ enabled?: boolean; itemsPerArray?: number }` | `{ enabled: true, itemsPerArray: 5 }` | Controls array truncation behavior. |
| `className` | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Toast notification system using the Sonner library, themed to match your design system.

## Installation

```bash
npx @e412/rnui-cli add sonner
```

## Import

```tsx
import { Toaster } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { toast } from 'sonner'
```

## Usage

Add the `Toaster` component to your root layout, then call `toast()` from anywhere in your app.

### Setup

```tsx
// In your root layout (e.g., layout.tsx)
import { Toaster } from '@e412/rnui-react'

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        {children}
        <Toaster />
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

### Triggering Toasts

```tsx
import { toast } from 'sonner'

<Button onClick={() => toast("Event has been created")}>
  Show Toast
</Button>
```

## Examples

### Success

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() => toast.success("Changes saved successfully")}
>
  Save
</Button>
```

### Error

```tsx
<Button
  variant="destructive"
  onClick={() => toast.error("Something went wrong")}
>
  Delete
</Button>
```

### Warning

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() => toast.warning("Please check your input")}
>
  Validate
</Button>
```

### Info

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() => toast.info("A new version is available")}
>
  Check Update
</Button>
```

### With Description

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() =>
    toast("Event Created", {
      description: "Your event has been scheduled for tomorrow at 10am.",
    })
  }
>
  Create Event
</Button>
```

### With Action

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() =>
    toast("File deleted", {
      action: {
        label: "Undo",
        onClick: () => console.log("Undo delete"),
      },
    })
  }
>
  Delete File
</Button>
```

### Loading / Promise

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() =>
    toast.promise(
      fetch("/api/data").then((res) => res.json()),
      {
        loading: "Loading data...",
        success: "Data loaded!",
        error: "Failed to load data",
      }
    )
  }
>
  Fetch Data
</Button>
```

### Custom Position

```tsx
// Place the Toaster at the top-center
<Toaster position="top-center" />
```

## Props

### Toaster

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `position` | `"top-left" \| "top-center" \| "top-right" \| "bottom-left" \| "bottom-center" \| "bottom-right"` | `"bottom-right"` | Toast position on screen |
| `expand` | `boolean` | `false` | Expand toasts by default |
| `richColors` | `boolean` | `false` | Use rich colors for toast types |
| `closeButton` | `boolean` | `false` | Show close button on toasts |
| `duration` | `number` | `4000` | Auto-dismiss duration in ms |
| `theme` | `"light" \| "dark" \| "system"` | `"system"` | Theme (auto-detected from next-themes) |

### toast() Function

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `toast(message)` | Show a default toast |
| `toast.success(message)` | Show a success toast |
| `toast.error(message)` | Show an error toast |
| `toast.warning(message)` | Show a warning toast |
| `toast.info(message)` | Show an info toast |
| `toast.loading(message)` | Show a loading toast |
| `toast.promise(promise, options)` | Show loading/success/error based on promise |
| `toast.dismiss(id?)` | Dismiss a specific or all toasts |

A tabbed interface for organizing content into switchable panels, built on Base UI's Tabs primitive.

## Installation

```bash
npx @e412/rnui-cli add tabs
```

## Import

```tsx
import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
<Tabs defaultValue="account">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="password">Password</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="account">Account settings here.</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="password">Change your password here.</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

## Examples

### Default Variant

```tsx
<Tabs defaultValue="overview">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="overview">Overview</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="analytics">Analytics</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="reports">Reports</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="overview">Overview content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="analytics">Analytics content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="reports">Reports content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

### Line Variant

```tsx
<Tabs defaultValue="overview">
  <TabsList variant="line">
    <TabsTrigger value="overview">Overview</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="analytics">Analytics</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="reports">Reports</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="overview">Overview content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="analytics">Analytics content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="reports">Reports content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

### Vertical Orientation

```tsx
<Tabs defaultValue="general" orientation="vertical">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="general">General</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="security">Security</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="notifications">Notifications</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="general">General settings</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="security">Security settings</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="notifications">Notification preferences</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

### With Icons

```tsx
import { UserIcon, LockIcon, BellIcon } from 'lucide-react'

<Tabs defaultValue="account">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="account">
      <UserIcon />
      Account
    </TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="security">
      <LockIcon />
      Security
    </TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="notifications">
      <BellIcon />
      Notifications
    </TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="account">Account settings</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="security">Security settings</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="notifications">Notification settings</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

### Disabled Tab

```tsx
<Tabs defaultValue="active">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="active">Active</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="disabled" disabled>Disabled</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="other">Other</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="active">Active tab content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="other">Other tab content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

## Props

### Tabs

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `defaultValue` | `any` | — | Initial active tab (uncontrolled) |
| `value` | `any` | — | Controlled active tab |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: any) => void` | — | Callback when active tab changes |
| `orientation` | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"` | `"horizontal"` | Layout orientation |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional CSS classes |

Accepts all props from Base UI `Tabs.Root`.

### TabsList

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `"default" \| "line"` | `"default"` | Visual variant; `"line"` shows an underline indicator |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional CSS classes |

### TabsTrigger

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `any` | — | Value identifying this tab (required) |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disable this tab |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional CSS classes |

Accepts all props from Base UI `Tabs.Tab`.

### TabsContent

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `any` | — | Value identifying which tab this panel belongs to (required) |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional CSS classes |

Accepts all props from Base UI `Tabs.Panel`.

A tree view component powered by headless-tree for rendering hierarchical data with expand/collapse, selection, drag-and-drop, and search support.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes @headless-tree/core @headless-tree/react
```

## Import

```tsx
import { Tree, TreeItem, TreeItemLabel, TreeDragLine } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { useTree } from '@headless-tree/react'

const items = {
  root: { name: "Root", children: ["src", "public"] },
  src: { name: "src", children: ["app", "lib"] },
  public: { name: "public", children: ["favicon"] },
  app: { name: "app.tsx" },
  lib: { name: "lib.ts" },
  favicon: { name: "favicon.ico" },
}

function MyTree() {
  const tree = useTree({
    rootItemId: "root",
    getItemName: (item) => item.name,
    isItemFolder: (item) => !!item.children,
    dataLoader: {
      getItem: (id) => items[id],
      getChildren: (id) => items[id].children ?? [],
    },
  })

  return (
    <Tree tree={tree}>
      {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
        <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
          <TreeItemLabel />
        </TreeItem>
      ))}
    </Tree>
  )
}
```

## Examples

### Chevron Toggle Icons

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree} toggleIconType="chevron">
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
</Tree>
```

### Plus/Minus Toggle Icons

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree} toggleIconType="plus-minus">
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
</Tree>
```

### Custom Indent

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree} indent={32}>
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
</Tree>
```

### With Drag Line

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree}>
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
  <TreeDragLine />
</Tree>
```

Data visualization components powered by Apache ECharts with automatic shadcn theme integration.

## Overview

The chart components wrap [Apache ECharts](https://echarts.apache.org/) with React bindings and automatic shadcn/ui theme integration. Charts automatically adapt to light and dark mode, use your design system's color tokens (`--chart-1` through `--chart-5`), and include sensible defaults via the built-in preset.

## Installation

ECharts is a peer dependency. Install it alongside the component library:

```bash
npm install echarts
```

Or with pnpm:

```bash
pnpm add echarts
```

## Import

```tsx
import { EChart } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Base Component

The `EChart` component manages the full ECharts instance lifecycle -- initialization, option updates, theme switching, resize handling, and cleanup.

```tsx
import { EChart } from '@e412/rnui-react'

<EChart
  option={{
    xAxis: { type: 'category', data: ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed'] },
    yAxis: { type: 'value' },
    series: [{ type: 'bar', data: [120, 200, 150] }],
  }}
  height={350}
/>
```

## Theme Integration

Charts automatically detect whether your app is in light or dark mode by watching for the `.dark` class on `<html>`. The built-in `shadcn-light` and `shadcn-dark` themes map your CSS custom properties (`--chart-1` through `--chart-5`, `--background`, `--foreground`, `--border`, etc.) to ECharts theme values.

You can also pass a specific theme:

```tsx
<EChart option={option} theme="dark" />
<EChart option={option} theme="light" />
```

## Preset

The `preset` prop (enabled by default) applies minimal, opinionated defaults that match shadcn styling -- clean axis lines, subtle grid lines, proper spacing, and tooltip styling. Disable it for raw ECharts behavior:

```tsx
<EChart option={option} preset={false} />
```

## Available Charts

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| [BarChart](/charts/bar-chart) | Vertical and horizontal bar charts |
| [LineChart](/charts/line-chart) | Line charts with optional smoothing |
| [AreaChart](/charts/area-chart) | Area charts with gradient fills |
| [PieChart](/charts/pie-chart) | Pie and donut charts |
| [RadarChart](/charts/radar-chart) | Radar/spider charts |
| [ScatterChart](/charts/scatter-chart) | Scatter plot charts |

## Configuration

Use the [chartConfig()](/charts/chart-config) builder API to programmatically create chart options with a fluent interface.

## Props (EChart)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `option` | `EChartsCoreOption` | — | ECharts option object |
| `width` | `number \| string` | `'100%'` | Chart width |
| `height` | `number \| string` | `350` | Chart height |
| `theme` | `string` | auto | Theme name (`'light'`, `'dark'`, or custom) |
| `preset` | `boolean` | `true` | Apply shadcn-style minimal defaults |
| `renderer` | `'canvas' \| 'svg'` | `'canvas'` | Rendering engine |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Show loading indicator |
| `loadingOption` | `LoadingOption` | — | Loading indicator options |
| `onEvents` | `Record<string, Function>` | — | ECharts event handlers |
| `autoResize` | `boolean` | `true` | Auto-resize on container change |
| `animateOnMount` | `boolean` | `true` | Run mount animation sequence |
| `animateOnMountDelayMs` | `number` | `16` | Delay before mount animation |
| `notMerge` | `boolean` | `false` | Replace option instead of merging |
| `lazyUpdate` | `boolean` | `false` | Lazy update mode |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional CSS classes |
| `style` | `CSSProperties` | — | Inline styles |

## Ref

Access the ECharts instance via ref:

```tsx
import { useRef } from 'react'
import { EChart, type ChartRef } from '@e412/rnui-react'

function MyChart() {
  const chartRef = useRef<ChartRef>(null)

  return (
    <EChart
      ref={chartRef}
      option={option}
    />
  )
}

// chartRef.current.getEchartsInstance() — get raw ECharts instance
// chartRef.current.resize({ width, height }) — manually resize
```

A fluent builder API for creating ECharts options with shadcn-compatible defaults.

## Import

```tsx
import { chartConfig } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

The `chartConfig()` function returns a `ChartConfigBuilder` instance. Chain methods to define series, configure axes, and set options, then call `.build()` to produce the final ECharts option object.

```tsx
import { EChart, chartConfig } from '@e412/rnui-react'

const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['A', 'B', 'C'] })
  .tooltip()
  .build()

<EChart option={option} />
```

## Examples

### Multi-Series Line Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .line({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar'], name: 'Revenue', smooth: true })
  .line({ data: [80, 120, 100], name: 'Expenses', smooth: true })
  .legend()
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Donut Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .pie({
    data: [
      { name: 'Desktop', value: 65 },
      { name: 'Mobile', value: 30 },
      { name: 'Tablet', value: 5 },
    ],
    donut: true,
  })
  .tooltip('item')
  .legend()
  .build()
```

### Stacked Bar Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3'], name: 'Product A', stack: 'total' })
  .bar({ data: [80, 120, 100], name: 'Product B', stack: 'total' })
  .legend()
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Horizontal Bars

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [420, 310, 180], categories: ['React', 'Vue', 'Angular'], horizontal: true })
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Line with Area Fill

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .line({
    data: [120, 200, 150, 80, 210],
    categories: ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May'],
    name: 'Revenue',
    smooth: true,
    area: true,
    areaOpacity: 0.2,
  })
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Scatter Plot

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .scatter({ data: [[10, 8], [8, 7], [13, 8], [9, 9]], name: 'Group A' })
  .scatter({ data: [[6, 4], [4, 3], [12, 10]], name: 'Group B' })
  .legend()
  .tooltip('item')
  .build()
```

### Radar Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .radar({
    indicators: [
      { name: 'Speed', max: 100 },
      { name: 'Reliability', max: 100 },
      { name: 'Cost', max: 100 },
    ],
    series: [
      { name: 'Product A', value: [90, 70, 60] },
      { name: 'Product B', value: [60, 85, 90] },
    ],
  })
  .legend()
  .tooltip('item')
  .build()
```

### Gauge Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .gauge({ value: 72, name: 'Score', min: 0, max: 100 })
  .build()
```

### With Data Zoom

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: longDataArray, categories: longCategoryArray })
  .dataZoom(0, 50)
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Custom Colors and Grid

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['A', 'B', 'C'] })
  .colors(['#3b82f6', '#8b5cf6', '#ec4899'])
  .grid({ left: 20, right: 20, top: 30, bottom: 20 })
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Raw Option Merge

For anything not covered by the helper methods, use `.raw()` to merge arbitrary ECharts options:

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['A', 'B', 'C'] })
  .raw({ animation: false, backgroundColor: '#fafafa' })
  .build()
```

## API Reference

### Series Methods

| Method | Arguments | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `.bar(config)` | `{ data, name?, categories?, stack?, horizontal?, borderRadius?, barWidth? }` | Add a bar series |
| `.line(config)` | `{ data, name?, categories?, smooth?, area?, areaOpacity? }` | Add a line series |
| `.pie(config)` | `{ data, donut?, innerRadius?, outerRadius? }` | Add a pie/donut series |
| `.scatter(config)` | `{ data, name?, symbolSize? }` | Add a scatter series |
| `.radar(config)` | `{ indicators, series }` | Add a radar chart |
| `.gauge(config)` | `{ value, name?, min?, max? }` | Add a gauge chart |

### Configuration Methods

| Method | Arguments | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `.title(text, subtitle?)` | `string, string?` | Set chart title (ECharts-rendered) |
| `.legend(position?)` | `'top' \| 'bottom' \| 'left' \| 'right'` | Enable legend (default: `'bottom'`) |
| `.tooltip(trigger?, formatter?)` | `'axis' \| 'item'`, `string \| Function` | Enable tooltip (default trigger: `'axis'`) |
| `.dataZoom(start?, end?)` | `number, number` | Enable data zoom slider (default: `0`-`100`) |
| `.grid(margins)` | `{ left?, right?, top?, bottom? }` | Set custom grid margins |
| `.colors(palette)` | `string[]` | Set custom color palette |
| `.raw(opts)` | `Partial<EChartsOption>` | Merge arbitrary ECharts options |
| `.build()` | — | Build the final `EChartsOption` object |
